title: 23 U.S.C. § 119 - National Highway Performance Program
content: 23 United States Code Section 119 - National Highway Performance Program. This section establishes the formula and requirements for federal highway funding. Subsection (a): Purpose. The purpose of the national highway performance program is to provide support for the condition and performance of the National Highway System, for the construction of new facilities on the National Highway System, and to ensure that investments of Federal-aid funds in highway construction are directed to support progress toward the achievement of performance targets established in a State's asset management plan. Subsection (b): Eligible Projects. Funds apportioned to a State under this section may be obligated only for projects on an eligible facility that are consistent with the purposes described in subsection (a). Eligible facilities include the Interstate System, major urban and rural arterials, connectors to major intermodal facilities, and the defense network. Subsection (c): State Performance Management. Each State shall develop a risk-based asset management plan for the National Highway System to improve or preserve the condition of the assets and the performance of the system. Subsection (d): Apportionment Formula. Funds shall be apportioned as follows: (1) 63% based on lane miles on the Interstate System; (2) 29% based on vehicle miles traveled on the Interstate System; (3) 8% based on contributions to the Highway Trust Fund from commercial vehicles. Subsection (e): Redistribution. Any amounts not obligated by a State within three years shall be redistributed to other States that have demonstrated ability to obligate funds efficiently. This ensures optimal use of federal highway resources and prevents funding delays.
